title: Analysis: Assessing Inflation’s Impact
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Inflation raises living costs, influences interest rates, and affects investment decisions, with energy and shelter being major drivers. Persistent inflation im
excerpt: Inflation raises living costs, influences interest rates, and affects investment decisions, with energy and shelter being major drivers. Persistent inflation impacts economic recovery and market volatility. Federal Reserve's inflation target is monitored closely by investors.

content.introduction: Inflation raises living costs, influences interest rates, and affects investment decisions, with energy and shelter being major drivers. Persistent inflation impacts economic recovery and market volatility. Federal Reserve's inflation target is monitored closely by investors.
body: While the pace of rising inflation is slowing, persistently higher prices continue to weigh on consumers and policymakers alike.

Current inflation affects the [economy](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/economic-recovery-status.html) by raising the cost of living, influencing interest rates, and shaping investment decisions. [1](#1 "Footnote 1") Investors also watch the Bureau of Economic Analysis’ Personal Consumption Expenditure price index because the [Federal Reserve](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/federal-reserve-tapering-asset-purchases.html) uses it in defining its inflation target, and February’s PCE price index rose 2.8% from a year earlier. [2](#2 "Footnote 2") More recently, investors have focused on what comes next as oil transportation constraints in the [Middle East](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/us-stock-market-volatility-continues.html) push energy prices higher and raise concern about future inflation. Even so, inflation remains high enough that [interest rate](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/federal-reserve-interest-rate.html) expectations can change quickly as new data arrives. Energy drove most of March’s jump in prices, but [shelter](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/investing-insights/interest-rates-impact-on-housing-market.html) remains a major source of ongoing pressure. Inflation remains a key driver of interest rates and [market volatility](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/is-a-market-correction-coming.html), and tariffs and energy shocks can create short-term setbacks even when the longer-term trend is improving.
This analysis is from the point of view of a US investor, so returns are conditioned using a measure of inflation expectations developed by the Federal Reserve.
